content:
## Choosing a Business Checking Option

When a customer asks which business checking product is likely to cost less, base the answer on lowest_projected_monthly_fee_after_workflow_fit. Do not recommend only by headline monthly fee; include whether the customer's expected workflow can meet the applicable waiver path. For side-by-side product attributes, see "Business Checking Product Comparison." For waiver mechanics, see "Business Checking Fee Waiver Policy."

## Opening Eligibility

A business checking account may be opened only when the business checking eligibility set is satisfied: business_registered, authorized_signer_has_authority, initial_deposit_at_or_above_product_minimum, no_unresolved_negative_balance_flag. Treat these as required conditions, not preferences. If any one item is missing from the conversation, ask a focused follow-up before describing the account as openable.

## Fee Waiver Questions

Answer waiver questions by tying the customer's stated activity to the product's waiver rules, then explain that the recommended account is the one with the lowest projected monthly fee after checking workflow fit. If the customer has not provided the expected balance, transaction pattern, or opening-deposit readiness needed to evaluate fit, do not assume it. Direct employees handling eligibility review to "Internal: Business Account Opening Eligibility" when the question moves from explanation to account-opening readiness.
title: Business Fee Waiver FAQ
